description_long: This object is a vintage portable stereo sound system, likely a record player or turntable, with a detachable lid that doubles as a speaker. The main body features a circular turntable platter, a tonearm, and several control knobs and buttons arranged on a cream-colored panel. The lid, which sits adjacent to the main unit, has a large circular speaker grille with a mesh pattern and a small handle for carrying. The exterior is finished in a brown and beige color scheme, with visible metal hardware and plastic components. The overall design is boxy and compact, suitable for transport and use in various urban settings such as parks, sidewalks, or garages. The materials suggest a combination of plastic, metal, and possibly wood for structural support.
description: A vintage portable stereo sound system with a brown and beige boxy design, featuring a turntable, control knobs, and a detachable lid with a speaker grille.
description_view_0: Side view showing the turntable with tonearm and control panel on the left, and the speaker lid with grille on the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the speaker grille on the lid and the control panel layout on the main unit.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the speaker lid and the rear panel of the main unit, including visible ports and connectors.
description_view_3: Opposite front view focusing on the turntable platter and tonearm, with the speaker lid in the background.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: Circular mesh grille with a fine perforated pattern, surrounded by a raised border.
attribute_control_panel_layout: Cluster of round knobs and buttons on the top left of the main unit, adjacent to the turntable platter, with no visible labeling.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
